title: Wiki vs. Zettelkasten — tradeoffs
body_markdown:
## Wiki vs. Zettelkasten — tradeoffs

Both are graph-shaped personal knowledge stores. They differ on:

| Axis | Zettelkasten | Wiki |
|------|-------------|------|
| Granularity | One idea per note | One *topic* per page |
| Linking | Dense, serendipitous | Hierarchical + cross-ref |
| Maintenance | You | LLM (in the Karpathy model) |
| Entry cost | High | Medium |

### Where I've landed
For a *personal* system I want the wiki shape: topic pages that the
LLM maintains, with a raw-notes layer underneath. Zettelkasten's
atomicity is beautiful but demands a kind of discipline I don't have
when I'm capturing on my phone in a grocery store.

The LLM absorbs the discipline gap. That's the whole pitch.
